summary: Third-batch fixes testing — 10 NEW Phase-2 items. 8/10 PASS, 1 not-fully-testable (FIX 9 requires connected WP site; modal short-circuits to no-site state on fresh user — same caveat from iteration 9), 1 partial (FIX 11 retry-btn cannot fire because the rendered article fallback uses status='PUBLISHED' for unknown ids). CRITICAL ISSUE discovered: a brand-new signup user triggers an uncaught React runtime error 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {id, name, monthlyPrice, annualPrice, description, articlesPerMonth, socialPostsPerMonth, ... plan-object fields})' which blocks the dashboard with a dev-overlay until dismissed. Existing iteration-9 user (test_1779193655@jalwa.com) does NOT trigger it — so the regression is specific to fresh signups (likely a plan object being rendered as React child in some code path that runs on first-paint for new users).

minor.issue: Returns no data for fresh user — frontend falls back to user's plan label (status='free'). No crash.
Returns no rows for fresh user — frontend renders user-activity-empty correctly.
Returns empty list — bell dropdown shows notifications-empty correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught runtime error 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {id, name, monthlyPrice, annualPrice, description, articlesPerMonth, socialPostsPerMonth, aiScansPerMonth, teamSeats, cmsConnections, brandVoiceModel, competitorComparison, prioritySupport, whiteLabel, isActive, sortOrder, createdAt, updatedAt})'. Reproducible 100% with brand-new signup (e.g. test_<ts>@jalwa.com / TestPassword123!). Iteration-9 user (test_1779193655@jalwa.com) does NOT trigger it, so something is being rendered conditionally for users whose subscription object includes the full Plan document. Likely culprit: SettingsPage/BillingTab or DashboardPage rendering {user.plan} or {subscription.plan} (which is now a Plan object) directly as JSX text. Dismissing the overlay reveals the rest of the UI works, but the overlay blocks all user interaction on prod-build the moment it appears.

Selectors '[data-testid*=rating]' returned 0 elements — rating control is present visually but either has no data-testid attribute or uses a different naming. Empty-submit toast (FIX 6 spec) does fire correctly. Cannot confirm whether the happy-path submission renders feedback-success because rating couldn't be set.

integration_issues.issue: Fresh user without a connected site → modal short-circuits to wp-no-site state. Steps 1/2/3 are NOT rendered, so [data-testid=wp-troubleshoot-toggle] is unreachable. Same caveat as iteration 9 (FIX A.1) for wp-download-plugin. Tip toggle code exists in WordPressConnectModal.jsx per files_of_reference; cannot exercise without a connected WP site.

Navigating /dashboard/auto-publish/article/test-failed-id renders an article with status='PUBLISHED' (frontend default fallback when backend returns no real article for that id). Because retry-btn only renders when status==='FAILED', it never appears for this id. To actually exercise the retry path, an article with status==='FAILED' must exist in the backend OR the frontend needs to honour the id literally and treat 'test-failed-id' as a FAILED stub. Calendar pills observed in DOM: PUBLISHED (green), SCHEDULED (gray), READY TO PUBLISH (blue). DRAFT/PUBLISHING/FAILED tokens are NOT visible in current calendar because no article currently has those states — the CSS classes exist in PublishPage.jsx but cannot be visually verified without seeded data.

issues: Cookie-consent banner overlay or form-loading state intercepts pointer events on hover during automated tests — only forgot-password was affected; login & signup hover validated cleanly. Likely cosmetic / test-only.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix 'Objects are not valid as a React child' regression for fresh signups. Search for any JSX that renders {user.plan}, {subscription.plan}, or {plan} directly without extracting .name. Most likely in SettingsPage (Billing tab) or in a default-plan display when no subscription exists. Reproducible: signup new user → land on /dashboard → red error overlay appears.
Add data-testid to feedback rating control (e.g. data-testid=feedback-rating-{n}) so the happy-path POST /api/feedback can be automated end-to-end.

(Optional) Seed at least one article with status='FAILED' in the demo data so /dashboard/auto-publish/article/test-failed-id renders article-retry-btn and the DRAFT/PUBLISHING/FAILED status pills are visible in the calendar.

(Carry-over from iteration 9) WP modal troubleshoot tips + wp-download-plugin still gated behind 'connect a site first'. Consider rendering steps regardless of site state, or seed a fake connected site for first-run users.
critical_code_review_comments: The 'Objects not valid as React child' error pattern with plan-object keys ({monthlyPrice, annualPrice, articlesPerMonth, ...}) is a classic mismatch between expected string 'Free' and actual Plan document. Backend appears to have started returning full Plan documents in subscription/user payloads; frontend should always read plan.name (or plan.id) instead of plan.
FIX 8 partial: when subscription is null/free, next-billing-date is correctly omitted but status-badge still renders with label 'free'. Good defensive UX.
FIX 10 case-insensitive email match works as specified (uppercase EMAIL enabled the confirm button). Disabled-by-default + password required is correctly enforced.
test_credentials: End-user (existing from iteration 9): test_1779193655@jalwa.com / TestPassword123!. A fresh signup test_1779264893@jalwa.com / TestPassword123! was created and reproduced the React-child crash. Admin: jalwa / jalwaisadmin.
seed_data_creation: 1 fresh end-user signup (test_1779264893@jalwa.com) — used to surface the new-user-only React render crash.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PHASE-2 results: FIX 1 PASS (8 coming-soon testids on /connections, 14 .coming-soon-card; CMS Connections & Social Accounts tabs likewise marked). FIX 2 PASS for /login + /signup primary & google buttons (bg=rgb(15,110,86), opacity=1, color=white on hover); /forgot-password hover blocked by overlay in automation but CSS class is identical so consider PASS. FIX 5 PASS (bell + dropdown + empty + View-all → /dashboard/notifications page). FIX 6 PASS (public /feedback page, footer link, dashboard FAB, dialog, empty-submit toast). Rating control needs data-testid for full happy-path. FIX 7 PASS (event-driven plan-limit-dialog with 50/Starter/articles copy; view-plans → /pricing; later closes). FIX 8 PASS (billing-subscription-card, subscription-status-badge, cancel-subscription-btn → cancel-subscription-dialog; next-billing-date hidden for free plan — correct). FIX 9 not-fully-testable on fresh user — modal short-circuits to wp-no-site (same caveat as iteration 9). FIX 10 PASS (danger-zone + delete-account-dialog flow + case-insensitive email match + password gate, confirm-delete-account-btn enables only when both match). FIX 11 partial — retry-btn code path exists but cannot fire because backend has no FAILED article; calendar pills DRAFT/PUBLISHING/FAILED unverified visually for the same reason. FIX 14 PASS (activity tab + user-activity-card + user-activity-empty when endpoint returns no rows). CRITICAL new-user crash must be fixed before next iteration — every fresh signup hits the React child error overlay on /dashboard.
rca_of_the_issue: Fresh signup → /dashboard → React render error. Stack trace points to throwOnInvalidObjectType in React reconciler. Found-object keys ({id, name, monthlyPrice, annualPrice, description, articlesPerMonth, socialPostsPerMonth, aiScansPerMonth, teamSeats, cmsConnections, brandVoiceModel, competitorComparison, prioritySupport, whiteLabel, isActive, sortOrder, createdAt, updatedAt}) are exactly a Plan document from /api/admin/plans. Some component fetches plan info for a new user (likely via billingApi.subscription or userApi.me) and the value is rendered directly inside JSX as a child (e.g. {subscription.plan}) instead of {subscription.plan?.name}. Existing iteration-9 user does NOT crash because /api/auth/me returns subscription:null for that user. Fresh signups apparently get a default/auto-attached subscription object containing the Plan document inline. Mitigation: (a) read plan.name everywhere, or (b) ensure backend returns subscription.plan as a string id and resolve client-side, or (c) wrap rendering with String() / optional chaining. Reproduction: signup test_@jalwa.com / TestPassword123! → land on /dashboard → dev overlay appears immediately.
